Hour.description: UTC Hour of the day in 24 hour format
Hour.name: hour

eta.description: Eta (arcsec) - easterly component of deflection of vertical.

xi.description: Xi (arcsec) - northerly component of deflection of vertical.

info.description:
The gravitational field of the earth is non-uniform. We provide web API access to various gravitational models used widely in:
  • Geodesy and Surveying: Accurate determination of geoid heights, crucial for precise GPS positioning.
  • Satellite Orbits and Navigation: Enhanced accuracy in orbit prediction and positioning of satellites.
  • Climate and Oceanography: Improved understanding of ocean circulation patterns and sea level variations.
  • Resource Exploration: Support for mineral and hydrocarbon exploration through accurate gravity field information.
  • Global Positioning and Mapping: Improved accuracy of global positioning systems (GPS) for navigation and mapping applications.
  • Academic and Research Applications: Support for academic research in fields like geophysics, geodesy, and atmospheric sciences.


Explore available endpoints via links on the left hand side. API requests must contain a key "API-Key" in the header (see code samples).

Visit our to obtain a key with a 14 day free trial.

Amentum Pty Ltd is not responsible nor liable for any loss or damage of any sort incurred as a result of using the API.

Copyright Amentum Pty Ltd 2024.
info.title: Gravity API

get.description: for a given latitude / longitude. The geoid is the shape the ocean surface would take if only gravity and the rotation of the Earth were considered. The geoid is the surface that defines zero elevation. The geoid height is the difference between an ideal reference ellipsoid and the geoid.

x-codeSamples.source: curl -X GET "https://gravity.amentum.io/egm2008/geoid_height?latitude=-45&longitude=45" -H "API-Key: <your_key>" -H  "accept: application/json"

library(httr)

url <- "https://gravity.amentum.io/egm2008/geoid_height"
response <- GET(url, add_headers(`API-Key` = "<your_key>", accept = "application/json"), 
                query = list(latitude = "-45", longitude = "45"))

content <- content(response)
cat(sprintf("Height: %s %s\n", content$height$value, content$height$units))

using HTTP
using JSON3

url = "https://gravity.amentum.io/egm2008/geoid_height"
headers = ["API-Key" => "<your_key>", "Accept" => "application/json"]
params = Dict("latitude" => "-45", "longitude" => "45")

response = HTTP.get(url, headers=headers, query=params)
json = JSON3.read(String(response.body))
println("Height: ", json.height.value, " ", json.height.units)

package main

import (
  "fmt"
  "net/http"
  "io/ioutil"
  "net/url"
)

func main() {
  baseUrl := "https://gravity.amentum.io/egm2008/geoid_height"
  params := url.Values{}
  params.Add("latitude", "-45")
  params.Add("longitude", "45")
  requestUrl := fmt.Sprintf("%s?%s", baseUrl, params.Encode())

  req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", requestUrl, nil)
  req.Header.Add("API-Key", "<your_key>")
  req.Header.Add("accept", "application/json")

  client := &http.Client{}
  resp, _ := client.Do(req)
  defer resp.Body.Close()
  body, _ := ioutil.ReadAll(resp.Body)

  fmt.Println(string(body))
}

get.description: for a given latitude / longitude. The gravity anomaly is the difference between the acceleration due to gravity on the Earth's surface and the value calculated assuming the reference ellipsoid.

x-codeSamples.source: curl -X GET "https://gravity.amentum.io/egm2008/gravity_anomaly?latitude=-45&longitude=45"  -H "accept: application/json" -H "API-Key: <your_key>"

library(httr)

url <- "https://gravity.amentum.io/egm2008/gravity_anomaly"
response <- GET(url, add_headers(`API-Key` = "<your_key>"), query = list(latitude = "-45", longitude = "45"))
content <- content(response, "parsed")
print(content$gravity_anomaly$value)

using HTTP
using JSON

url = "https://gravity.amentum.io/egm2008/gravity_anomaly"
headers = Dict("API-Key" => "<your_key>")
params = Dict("latitude" => "-45", "longitude" => "45")

response = HTTP.get(url, query=params, headers=headers)
println(String(response.body))

get.description:
for a given latitude, longitude, and date. The tidal gravity effect refers to the influence of gravitational forces, specifically from the Moon and the Sun. This endpoint implements a tidal effect formula from Longman IM (1959) "Formulas for computing the tidal accelerations due to the Moon and the Sun." J Geophys Res 64(12):2351–2355. https://doi.org/10.1029/JZ064i012p02351

The backend code was originally developed for Gravimeterra (contact gravimeterra@gmail.com). The default value of amplitude (1.175) was chosen to agree with GravSur, Amarante, Rogério Rodrigues. "Sistematização do processamento de dados gravimétricos aplicados à determinação do modelo geoidal". Technical report, 2012. PhD thesis.
get.summary: Calculate tidal effect using the Longman tide formula

x-codeSamples.source: curl -X GET "https://gravity.amentum.io/longman/tidal_effect?latitude=-45&longitude=45&year=2024&month=4&day=29&hour=7&minute=30"  -H "accept: application/json" -H "API-Key: <your_key>"

import requests
response = requests.get(
    "https://gravity.amentum.io/longman/tidal_effect",
    params={
        "latitude": -45,
        "longitude": 45,
        "year": 2024,
        "month": 4,
        "day": 29,
        "hour": 7,
        "minute": 30
    },
    headers={"accept": "application/json", "API-Key": "<your_key>"}
)
print(response.json())

library(httr)
response <- GET("https://gravity.amentum.io/longman/tidal_effect", 
                query=list(latitude=-45, longitude=45, year=2024, month=4, day=29, hour=7, minute=30),
                add_headers("accept" = "application/json", "API-Key" = "<your_key>"))
print(content(response, "parsed"))

using HTTP
response = HTTP.get("https://gravity.amentum.io/longman/tidal_effect",
                    query=Dict("latitude" => -45, "longitude" => 45, "year" => 2024, "month" => 4, "day" => 29, "hour" => 7, "minute" => 30),
                    headers=Dict("accept" => "application/json", "API-Key" => "<your_key>"))
println(String(response.body))

import Foundation
var urlComponents = URLComponents(string: "https://gravity.amentum.io/longman/tidal_effect")!
urlComponents.queryItems = [
    URLQueryItem(name: "latitude", value: "-45"),
    URLQueryItem(name: "longitude", value: "45"),
    URLQueryItem(name: "year", value: "2024"),
    URLQueryItem(name: "month", value: "4"),
    URLQueryItem(name: "day", value: "29"),
    URLQueryItem(name: "hour", value: "7"),
    URLQueryItem(name: "minute", value: "30")
]
var request = URLRequest(url: urlComponents.url!)
request.addValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "accept")
request.addValue("<your_key>", forHTTPHeaderField: "API-Key")
let task = URLSession.shared.dataTask(with: request) { data, response, error in
    if let data = data {
        print(String(decoding: data, as: UTF8.self))
    }
}
task.resume()

tags.description: Calculate geoid height and gravity anomaly values using the EGM2008 model. The official Earth Gravitational Model EGM2008 was developed and released to the public by the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA).
tags.name: egm2008

tags.description: The Longman formula calculates the gravitational effects of tidal forces from the Moon and Sun. This is a crucial factor for compensating gravity measurements for temporal variations as well as understanding small deformations or movements in the Earth's solid surface due to gravitational forces.
tags.name: longman
